mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor income which boosts aggregate consumption and corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 55% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month during the test period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25 percent, the zero lower bound constraint on conventional monetary policy amplifies the discount rate compression from forward guidance, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative or flat (annualized Sharpe < 0) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in industrial production and an unemployment rate above 6.5 percent signals a productivity-driven expansion with ample labor slack, which expands corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 55% of joint trigger instances, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a two-factor regression versus single-factor models.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales during the post-financial-crisis demand normalization period signals durable consumer deleveraging and rising household formation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS do not precede positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 58% of observations during post-crisis rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts exceeding 800 thousand units signals a durable construction cycle recovery that lifts earnings in housing-sensitive sectors and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 55% of months when HOUST exceeds 800 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production while the federal funds rate is below 1 percent signals a liquidity-fueled supply expansion where cheap capital amplifies capacity utilization gains, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with supply recovery
failure_condition: The joint condition does not produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or a regression interaction term between INDPRO changes and FEDFUNDS level is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals accelerating labor market tightening that boosts wage income expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns following a 0.2pp drop in UNRATE over 3 months are not positive in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index supports nominal corporate earnings growth and reduces real debt burdens, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL do not precede positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.
